Alison Jolly papers, circa 1919-2014

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Alison Jolly papers, circa 1919-2014
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8517
Abstract:
Professional papers, include diaries; correspondence; notes and notebooks; articles and reviews; prints; and photographs.
Creator:
Jolly, Alison.
Quanitities:
25 cubic feet.
264 gigabytes.
Language:
Collection material in English
A.J, 1
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Alison Bishop Jolly graduated from Cornell in 1958 and received a Ph.D. from Yale University in 1962. As a post-doctoral student at Yale, she pioneered in-depth field research on the behavior and ecology of lemurs in Madagascar, becoming an authority on the ring-tailed lemur. She worked as a research assistant at the New York Zoological Society, and after moving to England as a research associate at Cambridge and the University of Sussex. From 1992 to 1996, she was president of the International Primatological Society, winning its lifetime achievement award in 2010. She was also appointed an officer in the National Order of Madagascar, and in 2006, a newly-discovered mouse lemur species for named in her honor: Microcebus jollyae.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Professional papers, include diaries; correspondence; notes and notebooks; articles and reviews; prints; and photographs.
Children's story books written by Alison Jolly and illustrated by Deborah Rossart as part of the Ako Project to involve and aid primary school teachers in Madagascar; also note cards by Deborah Rossart from the project. Also, woven fabric from Madagascar.
Access Restrictions:

Access to Boxes 17, 18, and 20 (Personal family papers) restricted until 2044.
Restrictions on Use:

Family retains copyright to be reviewed in 2024.
Conditions Governing Use

Permission to quote from or publish work based on the papers is restricted to permission of the family.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Alison Jolly Papers, #8517. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
SUBJECTS

Places:
Madagascar -- Description and travel.
Subjects:
Lemurs.
CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Series I. Personal Papers
Box 19 Folder 1-2
Letters to Alison at Yale Location: Yale
 1950-62
Scope and Contents
Floyd year out met at Perugia summer 1959, one of circle of friends/lovers. Dick, Marie Achille - Perugia. Idilko Hayes - bosom friend met on Brindisi-Athens ferry 1951, seen at intervals since Victoria cake recipe: "To Kek" made by Greek Maid Victoria 1951-2, staple diet but needs to be cooked in local baker's oven up at the corner of Neophyte Douka.
Box 19 Folder 1-2
Letters from Margaret Nichol Location: Yale
 1951-52
Scope and Contents
Margaret Nichol, friend 1951-52, niece of principal of Greek girls equivalent to Athens college.
Box 19 Folder 1-2
Letters from Lanier family Yale
 1956-57
Scope and Contents
Hosts for junior year in France 1956-57.
Box 19 Folder 3
Letters to Yale Yale
 1950s
Scope and Contents
AMK (mother), Jiffy - Janet Farnham, friend since childhood married to Ted Heitmann, Evelyn Hutchinson from Rome, Uncle Ted Bishop (Prudential Insurance salesman), Ann Caristi - Yale room mate 1958-59 left for Florence and married there.
Box 19 Folder 3
Photos Yale
 1950s
Scope and Contents
MGB as mace beaver, Cornell and over lawn- left to right AMKB, MGB, AMK, Bill and Betty Miller
Box 19 Folder 3
Letters to Yale
 1950-60
Scope and Contents
Eba, RJ, Kingsbury Chase cousin, H. Kunert, Quinto Maganini - Norfolk Symphony Orchestra, Fabrizio Melano. John Buettner-Janush, diary 1960, MGB, Lanier - president of the Transat, Sigrid - German room mate, Dick Thorington, letter from AMKB and break with Ursula Cowgill. Inaugeration of the France 1962. Conference organised by John Buettner-Janusch, N Y Academy Science 1962.
Box 19 Folder 4
Letters and memorabilia Cornell
 1954-58
Scope and Contents
Gail Beckman, Baldwin friend, later lawyer Margaretta Maganini marriage invitation Louisa DuPont Copeland, Cornell (Theta) friend Janet Rowntree, Cornell friend, later astronomer. Trauma - undergrad humour, MGB, Uncle Ted Bishop, Jiffy - Janet Farnham, Idiko Hayes, Mrs Mary Taber, AMK illustrated Mr Taber's book. Laura Melano (later professor of psychology), Genevieve Gautier - sister of E. Lanier, diary 1957-58
Box 19 Folder 4
Woods Hole Invertebrate Course
 1956
Postcard, letter and catalogue
Box 19 Folder 4
Dear Aunt Paulie Cornell
 1956?
Handwritten notes
Scope and Contents
Draft of novel? Written in Cornell, since sorority dance scenes. "My handwriting but I've suppressed memory of it."
Box 19 Folder 4
On the Norman Kingdom in Sicily Location: Cornell
 1957
Typed term paper
Scope and Contents
On Constanz of Sicily which should be a movie.
Box 19 Folder 5-6
Junior year in France France
 1956-57
Letters and envelopes
Scope and Contents
The Seacrests' probably holiday home of Kingsbury's pre WW1. AMK, uncle Ted Bishop, Penny (Eleanor) Sutherland - childhood friend, Margi Clulow, Aunt Ninky (Katherine K.K. Chase) Vatigan stamps from Easter trip to Italy 1957 with AMK, MGB, Francis and Virginia Scheetz (Cornell trustee) and Fabrizio. Ione Gyalistas - younger Baldwinite. Fabrizio and I scandalised her governess by taking her to the Lapin Agile on Montmartre. Janet Rowntree, Lisa Robinson, wife of Richard Robinson, Prof. of Oxford, long term friends of MGB and AMK (were at Cornell in wartime) Margaret Nicholl. Photos: Fabrizio Melano, Henri, Francois and Jean Lanier. Totality of my notes on "Le theme de Faust" Sorbonne course.
Box 19 Folder 6
Diary - junior year in France
 1956-57
Box 19 Folder 7
Drawing book - junior year in France
 1956-57
Scope and Contents
Drawing book with rather good picture of room of Lanier with Dagobert the dog, Eloi the cat and Cornell blazer.
Box 19 Folder 8
High school Diary
 1957
Scope and Contents
Diary of Scandinavian trip
Box 19 Folder 8
High school letters
 1950s
Letters, notebooks, photos and postcards
Scope and Contents
Unitarian church attended with parents, handwriting analysis, postcards from Finland - Scandinavian tour summer 1958, Ile de France 1953, graduation photos Baldwin school - AMK to school. Photos from Ithaca New Year's party with Dmitri Nabokov and Luigi Einaudi, profs sons. Letter about dear Victoria, maid in Athens - Berni Kuboszewski, Bill Sykes death 1950 (I'd been put in 10th grade English in case I couldn't take English in Athens) London 1951, writings at Baldwin, primary school poems by A.B. and Eleanor (Penny) Sutherland.
Box 19 Folder 9-10
high school diaries, primary school poems
 1953

Greece
 1951-52
Sketchbook, diaries and notebooks

original photo of Tupaia

Scope and Contents

maps

 2003

Box 15 Folder 2
World like our own - rough drafts Author: Alison Jolly Location:
 1975
typed drafts
Scope and Contents
Much cut later to take out personal parts. Originally encouraged by knopf editor to write it as a diary. After refusals by knopf and others, on the grounds that 'no one ever heard of Madagascar so not worth publishing' final version taken by Yale University Press.

1962-63

Box 19 Folder 11
Menus, de Grasse Location: Greece
 1951
menus
Scope and Contents
My parents wanted me to experience Europe as they had known it. They saw that almost all transatlantic crossings would soon be by plane. They chose to cross first class on the slowest French line ship (10 days to Portsmouth) the de Grasse, known for superb cuisine to make up for lack of speed. I longed to be in 3rd class where young people seemed to have fun. At least I got to dance with Alec Waugh (Evelyn's brother) at the captain's dinner though he eventually reconciled on asking my age. I was 14.

Box 24 Folder 3
Historic Maps of Madagascar Location: Madagascar
maps
Scope and Contents
1954 1:500,000 10 part series, post independence: 1 : 500,00 12 part series, Inst. Nat. de Geodesie et Cartographie, formations vegetales de Mad. 1: 100,000. PNUD, CI, ANGAP, E andF 1990s, Humbert, H and Cours - Darne 1964 Carte Internationle du Tapis Vegetal, Madagascr 1: 100,000 with inset maps of geology, soils etc. Inst. francais de Pondicherry based on air photos 1950. 1964 1: 500,000 12 part, Inst. Geographique de Paris, 1973 1:50,000, sheets for Ranomafana-Ifanadiana and Kianjavato.
